findings.rendered_ai_response: Korean greeting + Consensus onboarding: 'annyeonghaseyo! Consensus-e osin geol hwanyeonghamnida. Consensus-neun 2eok pyeon isang-ui yeongu nonmun-eul gibaneuro, jayeoneo jilmun-hamyeon gwallyeon nonmun-eul chaja yoyak-gwa insight-reul jegong-haneun haksul geomsaek enjin-imnida.' followed by bullet list of sample research topics (treatment effects, academic consensus, meta-analysis/RCT)
findings.schema_summary: Event-stream body uses 3 event kinds: (1) 'node_streaming' — per-token deltas, field: {type, node, delta}; 'node' is logical stream name like 'conversational_response_1'; 'delta' is a text fragment (often a single Korean jamo/word). (2) 'node_complete' — sent when one node finishes, {type, node, value}; value is typically empty {} for conversational responses. (3) 'agent_complete' — sent at end, {type} only. Total ~30 node_streaming frames for a 1-sentence greeting; event type labels in DevTools all start with 'a' (likely 'agent' or 'answer'). URL pattern encodes thread_id (slug from URL) + interaction_id (newly created per prompt). This endpoint is a true SSE, unlike the Next.js _next/data endpoint which is just page hydration.

mandatory_fields_status.raw_events: POPULATED (4 representative rows: 2 deltas, 1 node_complete, 1 agent_complete)

implications_for_apf_interception: Consensus has a CLEAN, simple SSE schema ideal for T1c envelope design. The 'delta' stream + 'node_complete' + 'agent_complete' pattern is minimal and predictable — much simpler than perplexity's backend_uuid+classifier_results envelope. An APF envelope targeting consensus would need: {type: 'node_streaming', node: 'conversational_response_1', delta: '[WARNING_TEXT_FRAGMENTS]'} per token, then {type: 'node_complete', node: 'conversational_response_1', value: {}}, then {type: 'agent_complete'}. No UUIDs needed in the envelope itself (they're in the URL path). This is the cleanest target observed this cycle, easier than baidu/duck/pplx.
